author: OE-GOD
name: SP4096 + Byte-Level PPM Adaptive-λ Mixture
technique_summary: Base: Kevin Clark's 2026-04-01 SP4096 + 11L + MLP4x + high WD + depth-recurrence + LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 + legal TTT + parallel residuals + EMA + GPTQ int6 + brotli (val_bpb 1.098 non-mixed). Addition: byte-level PPM-D order-5 with adaptive-λ gate mixed with the NN's per-token target logprob in byte-probability space during final sliding-window eval. Measured delta: −0.120 BPB.

mixture_technique.predictor: byte-level PPM-D order 5 (pure Python, online, legal TTT on already-scored val)
mixture_technique.mixing: adaptive λ gate: λ=0.05 when PPM max-symbol probability > 0.9, else λ=0.9
mixture_technique.byte_marginalization: spread NN token logprob uniformly across UTF-8 bytes (conserves total NN bits)
mixture_technique.measurement: NN-only and mixture BPB both measured on first 5,000,000 val tokens (16.42 MB byte stream) during the final sliding-window eval; NN-full (non-subsampled) sliding BPB reported alongside for audit

compliance.artifact_under_16mb_note: Raw train_gpt.py is 72 KB; we ship it as a 22 KB lzma+base85 exec-stub (same compression pattern used by several prior records). All 3 seeds ship artifacts in 15.96-15.98 MB range.
compliance.eval_under_600s_note: Sliding eval with mixture completed in 144-165 s (well under the 10-min cap). Pure-Python PPM on 5M-token subset.
compliance.no_ngram_cache_note: Byte-level online PPM predictor (not a precomputed n-gram cache). The PPM suffix-count table starts empty at eval start and is built from the same val tokens the NN has already scored. This is legal TTT per rules: 'you are only allowed to test-time train on validation set tokens you've already evaluated your model on, since those tokens have already been graded'.
